title: Watching End of Evangelion late at night is bad
display_title: Watching End of Evangelion late at night is bad
wikitext:
[]: Watching End of Evangelion late at night is bad             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Mon Aug 15, 2005 8:42 am I watched End of Evangelion at midnight, in utter silence. Let's just say at one point I actually screamed aloud and had to calm down for a few minutes. Add me up to the list of people that have been shaken by EoE. Also, now I am very, very scared of Rei. Also, I did NOT sleep well. :shock:

[]: Re: Watching End of Evangelion late at night is bad             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Mon Aug 15, 2005 8:51 am Takumi Fujiwara wrote: I watched End of Evangelion at midnight, in utter silence. Let's just say at one point I actually screamed aloud and had to calm down for a few minutes. Add me up to the list of people that have been shaken by EoE. Also, now I am very, very scared of Rei. Also, I did NOT sleep well. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Mon Aug 15, 2005 8:55 am Yeah, I had a wet dream about Eva last night, all right. I pissed my pants after dreaming about Rei. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Aug 15, 2005 10:11 am does that count as a wet dream? i was under the impression that a wet dream referred to a dream that ended with ejaculation

[]:             Quote Post by Hyper Shinchan » Mon Aug 15, 2005 10:47 am sadsadshinji wrote: does that count as a wet dream? i was under the impression that a wet dream referred to a dream that ended with ejaculation

[]:             Quote Post by The Sandman » Mon Aug 15, 2005 2:03 pm Takumi Fujiewara wrote: I watched End of Evanglion at midnight, in utter silence. Let's just say at one point I actually screamed aloud and had to calm down for a few minutes. Add me to the list of people that had been shaken by EoE. Also, now I am very, very scared of Rei. Also, I did NOT sleep well. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 15, 2005 2:30 pm Babies.

[]:             Quote Post by Hunter21 » Mon Aug 15, 2005 4:06 pm Hexon.Arq wrote: Babies.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Mon Aug 15, 2005 4:17 pm Once, I started playing EoE one my PC (in japenese, of course) and then started playing Diablo 2. It was...quite an odd experience. Especially when I realised that I knew what most of the lines were.

[]:             Quote Post by sadsadshinji » Mon Aug 15, 2005 4:20 pm well, after you see it a certain amount of times, its kind of natural... speaking of memorization, at a certain point, i remembered the opening sequence of the episodes

[]:             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Mon Aug 15, 2005 6:34 pm The Sandman wrote: Takumi Fujiewara wrote: I watched End of Evanglion at midnight, in utter silence. Let's just say at one point I actually screamed aloud and had to calm down for a few minutes. Add me to the list of people that had been shaken by EoE. Also, now I am very, very scared of Rei. Also, I did NOT sleep well. :shock:

[]: Re: Watching End of Evangelion late at night is bad             Quote Post by Machinehead » Mon Aug 15, 2005 6:58 pm Takumi Fujiwara wrote: I watched End of Evangelion at midnight, in utter silence. Let's just say at one point I actually screamed aloud and had to calm down for a few minutes. Add me up to the list of people that have been shaken by EoE. Also, now I am very, very scared of Rei. Also, I did NOT sleep well. :shock:

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Aug 15, 2005 7:51 pm When I first saw EoE, I was shocked at what I saw. But I didnt have a problem sleeping, at all.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Aug 15, 2005 7:57 pm EoE was like a shot of Jack Daniels, a shock to they system the first time, but it gets better after that.

[]:             Quote Post by Timesplitter 01 » Mon Aug 15, 2005 8:05 pm Thanks to EoE I am no longer shocked about anything on a visual format

[]:             Quote Post by Machinehead » Mon Aug 15, 2005 8:18 pm Timesplitter 01 wrote: Thanks to EoE I am no longer shocked about anything on a visual format

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Mon Aug 15, 2005 9:51 pm I watched Evangelion all in one sitting, so it was actually morning when I saw EoE.

[]: Re: Watching End of Evangelion late at night is bad             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Tue Aug 16, 2005 12:44 am Machinehead wrote: The whole Lilith's Egg/Uber Rei scene at the end is absolutely beautiful.

[]:             Quote Post by The Sandman » Tue Aug 16, 2005 2:46 am Takumui Fujiwara wrote: lol[list=][list]I had the same experience with Elfen Lied. Got scared shitless then after that I was immune to it. I'm gonna watch EoE again tonight.

[]:             Quote Post by Takumi Fujiwara » Tue Aug 16, 2005 2:49 am The Sandman wrote: Well I just got the first volume of Elfen Lied and after watching the first episode my reaction was HOLY SHIT!!!! MY GOD :shock: :shock: :shock: :shock:
